layman_headline: Crypto market is deep in fear territory — patience beats impulse right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' mode, with sentiment worsening by the day. Almost every altcoin on our watchlist shows bearish chart structure — prices below their key moving averages with no clear reversal signals yet. Bitcoin is holding relatively steady near $64,600 and dominating the market, which means money is flowing out of smaller coins. This is a classic 'wait for the dust to settle' environment rather than a time to chase entries.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines with cash ready. The best swing trades come when fear peaks and we see the first clear bounce signals — we're not there yet. Watch for ETH to test the $1,800 support level as a potential entry if it holds convincingly.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate shows bearish chart bias (-0.55 to -0.70), with most below all major moving averages. The RSI signal — our most reliable indicator at 80% win rate — flags TRUMP (RSI 22) as deeply oversold, but the coin has -97.8% from ATH with no chart support structure and terrible risk/reward (1.0x). ETH's RSI at 39 is approaching oversold with a massive +17.2% 5-day momentum suggesting a potential trend shift, but it hasn't reclaimed its SMA20 yet. BTC dominance at 56.6% is hostile for altcoin entries. The Fear & Greed at 25 and worsening suggests we haven't hit capitulation yet. With no volume confirmation, no MACD crosses, and no bullish candlestick reversals among any candidates, the probability-weighted play is to wait for cleaner setups.
risk_factors: Fear & Greed trend is still worsening — sentiment could deteriorate further before bottoming
BTC dominance at 56.6% actively suppresses altcoin recoveries — any BTC weakness drags alts down harder
Weekend geopolitical risk (oil supply concerns per CryptoSlate) could trigger sudden risk-off selling
watch_list: ETH holding $1,800 support — a convincing bounce with volume would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index — a reversal from 25 back above 30 would signal capitulation has passed
BTC reaction to $65,000 resistance and the massive call spreads targeting $72K by month-end
market_context: Extreme Fear (25/100) with a worsening trend. BTC stable near $64.6K with high dominance at 56.6%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. Weekend geopolitical risk around oil supply adds to uncertainty.
context_banner: Market fear is deepening with no reversal confirmation — capital preservation is the highest-conviction trade right now.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25 with a worsening weekly trend (avg 25.4) — no sign of bottoming yet, making premature entries risky.
Our most reliable indicator (80% win rate) shows ETH at 39 approaching oversold and TRUMP deeply oversold at 22, but no bounce confirmation exists on either.
Massive BTC call spreads targeting $72K by month-end ahead of the Fed meeting could provide the catalyst alts need, but the trade hasn't triggered yet.
Weekend geopolitical risk flagged — 20% of world's oil supply in the balance creates risk-off pressure that could hit crypto.
ETH derivatives reset noted, suggesting overleveraged positions have been flushed — a cleaner base for eventual recovery but timing uncertain.
